reviews.author: GOONER.
reviews.text: Located in An Giang province, Tra Su Cajuput Forest is a magnificent natural masterpiece that showcases the quintessential beauty of Southern Vietnam's flood season. Spanning over 800 hectares, this specialized forest is not only a biodiversity hotspot but also an enchanting destination for those seeking tranquility and a deep connection with nature.🇻🇳🇻🇳 ​The journey into the heart of the forest begins on a motorboat, but the true magic starts when you switch to a small wooden rowing boat, known as a "xuồng ba lá." As the boatman gently paddles through the emerald-green carpet of water lettuce, you will feel like you are gliding into a fairytale world. The canopy of towering cajuput trees creates a cool, shaded tunnel, while the sunlight filters through the leaves, shimmering on the water’s surface. The air is incredibly fresh, filled with the faint scent of cajuput flowers and the melodious symphony of thousands of birds nesting above. ​Tra Su is home to a vast array of flora and fauna, including rare species of storks and water birds. From the "Vong Canh" observation tower, you can use a telescope to capture the breathtaking panoramic view of the vast green forest, dotted with white storks and the majestic That Son mountain range in the distance. Another highlight is the "Bamboo Bridge of Ten Thousand Steps," which winds through the forest, offering a unique path to explore the wilderness on foot. ​The best time to visit is during the floating season, from September to November, when the water levels rise and the greenery is at its most vibrant. Tra Su Cajuput Forest is more than just a scenic spot; it is a serene sanctuary where the soul can find peace. Whether you are a photography enthusiast or a nature lover, a trip to this green paradise will undoubtedly leave you with unforgettable memories of the Mekong Delta’s authentic charm.❤❤❤

reviews.author: Nguyen Khanh Ngoc
reviews.text: If you’re looking for a place that perfectly captures Vietnam’s diverse forests and stunning natural beauty, this spot is an absolute must-visit. The entrance ticket costs 120,000 VND, with optional rides that make the experience even more memorable: a motorboat ride for 70,000 VND (round trip) and a rowing boat for another 70,000 VND (round trip). I highly recommend trying both! The motorboat takes you deep into the heart of the forest, where you can admire the vast scenery and peaceful atmosphere. Then, switch to the rowing boat — it glides through narrow forest paths, offering a closer, more intimate view of the surroundings. If you’re lucky, you might even spot a few birds up close! 🐦 On top of that, don’t miss the observation tower — the ticket costs only 5,000 VND, and it’s absolutely worth it! From the top (about five to six stories high), you’ll get a panoramic view of the forest stretching endlessly beneath you — a breathtaking sight you shouldn’t miss. 🌳✨ And here’s a fun bonus: the pigeons here are surprisingly friendly! You can buy a small pack of dried seeds for just 10,000 VND to feed them — some will even perch on your arms to grab a snack. It’s such a wholesome experience, and if you’re curious, check out my video below for a better look at the moment! 🎥✨ The staff here are super friendly, too. You’ll also find a few small souvenir stalls selling charming keepsakes like keychains, traditional Vietnamese costumes, and other handmade items — all at very affordable prices. I even got myself a mini coconut-leaf fan for just 40,000 VND

reviews.author: LLSJ
reviews.text: Beautiful and Tranquil. Apparently the water level had already dropped 1m from its highs during September and October however we still enjoyed the serenity. We did the Sampan boat ride and found it very relaxing. We just turned up and bought our tickets at the front gate, having arrived there at 7am from Chau Doc using a private driver. We must have been early enough to miss the tour groups as it felt like we had the rainforest to ourselves and it was still very cool.

reviews.author: LiLi Nguyen
reviews.text: Visiting Tra Su Cajuput Forest in An Giang was an unforgettable experience that brought you closer to the peaceful beauty of Vietnam’s Mekong Delta. This ecotourism destination is a perfect escape from the busy city life, offering a refreshing connection with nature and wildlife. You will floated through the green canals on a small boat, you was surrounded by a stunning carpet of water lilies and duckweed, with tall cajuput trees forming a natural canopy overhead. The sound of birds and the cool breeze made the journey feel magical. It was fascinating to see so many species of birds and other animals living freely in this natural sanctuary. It was especially beautiful at sunrise and sunset when the soft golden light reflected on the water.

reviews.author: Henry Saigon
reviews.text: This forest is such a gem! 🌳 The boat ride through the lush greenery was incredibly peaceful and beautiful. I loved seeing all the plants and enjoying the serene atmosphere. It's a perfect escape for nature lovers, and I highly recommend a visit. For a truly peaceful experience, visit between 3-4 PM! It's not too hot, and you'll witness countless birds returning to the forest. A rowing boat is a must for enjoying nature's serenity! 🌳

reviews.author: charlee
reviews.text: Beautiful forest with many birds and plants. We bought a combo ticket for 230K that included entry, paddle boat and motor boat. I really enjoyed how peaceful the paddle boat was. The motor boat was a little disappointing - rushed straight through the forest without taking any time to appreciate it. Many other boats were stopped for a long time to get pictures but we just zoomed straight past. They drop you to another part of the forest to explore and then take you back which was nice though. The entry point we put into maps was Thốt Nốt Sữa (Trà Sư) which had free motorbike parking. Definitely recommend going early in the morning.

reviews.author: Anthony Bailey
reviews.text: Loved our visit here. Took a 45 min moped from Chau Doc and jumped on the paddle boat to start with. Arriving at 7.30 meant we were the only people on the water, combine that serenity with the bird noise and it was a sensory pleasure. Then took the motorboat and the driver was exceptional at spotting birds and sights, slowing and stopping where needed. Memories to treasure forever.

reviews.author: philo sluijsmans
reviews.text: While its more a park then a reserve i still had a wonderfull time. Lots of birds to see, rest and quiet. Its possible to get boat tours, recommanded

reviews.author: Sam Stampelos
reviews.text: Tra Su Cajuput Forest is absolutely a must-visit destination. We booked our trip through our hotel in Chau Doc, which offered a convenient all-inclusive package including tickets and transportation. This was essential because the forest is quite remote—definitely not a place you want to navigate on your own. Our driver took us there, waited patiently during our visit, and brought us back to the hotel, making the whole experience stress-free and seamless. The entrance tickets were included in the package, so there were no hidden costs. The highlight of the visit is undoubtedly the boat ride through the serene waterways of the forest, which is part of the ticket price—no extra fees. The lush greenery, peaceful atmosphere, and unique landscape make Tra Su Cajuput Forest a truly mesmerizing and unforgettable experience. Highly recommended! PS: Do not forget your mosquito repellent.

reviews.author: Kathy Mclachlan
reviews.text: This was a beautiful place to see when in Chau Doc. Recommend using both boats and taking a return taxi to the site...is 40 mins from Chau Doc. Loads of birds to see+

reviews.author: Lưu Anh Tuân
reviews.text: Nice place to have some understanding about the ecology of swamp and wetland of Cửu Long delta. The services are excellents, the staffs bought great help. It will be perfect if the Tra Su Cajaput forest can add kayak for their services.

reviews.author: Robyn “Tweezledinky”
reviews.text: Incredible place. Apparently it was re-wilded in the 1980s and protected since 2004. Absolutely magical. The biodiversity and wildlife is amazing. The boat trips (one gentle canoe) and two long tail boat trips were fantastic. I could have spent all day just gently floating by. We were super lucky as it was really quiet. We only saw 2 other tourists on the delta that day highly recommend a visit.

reviews.author: Hằng
reviews.text: Although I was not interested in the scenery like this, it’s still worth visiting. You should visit it in the afternoon, then, you can see lots of birds. On the way to the forest from main gate, I found a bubble tea store, they had Palmyra palm milk tea which tasted good.

reviews.author: Thiam Siew Tan
reviews.text: Took the sampan ride. Very relaxing. Back to nature. Huge lotus plants. Don't follow to close the boat in front of you then you can't take nice photos.

reviews.author: Trang Ngô
reviews.text: It was a life-time memory for me, I have never seen that many birds and flowers before. It is definitely worthy to become one of the significant specialties in Vietnam.

reviews.author: Martian
reviews.text: If you are nature explorer, dont forget this place when you have chance to visit Chau Doc. The fauna ecosystem here is quite interesting.

reviews.author: Lauren Eade
reviews.text: My friend and I had the most wonderful day seeing so many egrets, herons and kingfishers in such a beautiful setting. We were surprised at how quiet the forest was with visitors, but the peace made our boat trip through the marshes even more special. Definitely recommend choosing the rowing boat option instead of the motorboat so as to not disturb the wildlife.

reviews.author: ste
reviews.text: Wonderful! My girlfriend and I arrived here with a private guide at 7 (Monday) when the place opened and we were completely alone, literally the first and behind us there was nobody. We did a tour with a small boat moved by hand with the quiet of the forest at a early moment, only the sound of the water and the sung of the birds could break the peace 😍 the. We went with a motor boat to another place to enjoy the canals and the forest view! Really recommended!

reviews.author: Samarth Solanki
reviews.text: Excellent place and real appreciation for the management team. They are providing amazing and well planned and executed amenities. And the eco system is very nicely preserved even after frequent crowds and tourism programs.

reviews.author: Devon Nguyen
reviews.text: Good experience. Entry ticket 100k so cheap, riding boat 60k. Friendly and helpful staff, willing to help elderly that difficult to walk with wheelchair. National conservative cajeput forest and birds area at Châu đốc province. Very beautiful during raising tide season of Mekong delta

reviews.author: Lan Price
reviews.text: It's very nice here. I love it. Good food too

reviews.author: Bao Anh
reviews.text: Tra Su Melaleuca Forest is one of the best bird conservation areas in Vietnam. Visitors will see flocks of egrets flying high above, and many egret nests perched on the melaleuca trees. There are also kingfishers, bitterns, waterfowl, and teals... If you take a rowboat or speedboat, you'll feel like you've stepped into a fairytale world, with the water covered in green water hyacinths and lotus flowers, and the slender, silent melaleuca trees.

reviews.author: Anna Bortsova
reviews.text: The park entrance has good infrastructure: Wi-Fi, ticket office, shops, parking. We travelled by taxi from chau doc and it was very straightforward ( didn’t need to book a tour). Park tickets only accepts cash. Recommend getting the speedboat ticket alongside park entry ticket. Great experience, you probably won’t spend more than 2 hours here unless you stay on for lunch. Tours there go out for 8am and 2pm. We went there for 11am ourselves and it was very quiet and peaceful. We were the only people in our boat. The park itself is beautiful - you can walk / cycle. There's a nice garden at the front and some good paths. There is an impressive bamboo bridge (though if you're bigger than the average local use common sense before embarking on it). We saw some birds, a little snake, lizards etc. The speedboat ride was fun (it lasts about 30mins). Our taxi driver offered to wait for us and then took us back to the hotel (taxi apps aren’t supported in this area)

reviews.author: Abdol Malik
reviews.text: Tra Su melaleuca forest is one of the most attractive place to travel at An Giang, suitable for half day trip! Noted that - no private way for wheel chair

reviews.author: Micah Rankin
reviews.text: Our family visited Tra Su forest and had an amazing time. I would highly recommend this to anyone. We took the paddle boat, speed boat and walked through the forest. We saw lots of beautiful birds. It was nice to see some nature in Vietnam that wasn’t affected by the plastic waste.

reviews.author: Riccardo Roma
reviews.text: The place is beautiful, entry is 100k/person and I recommend doing the paddle boat tour for 60k/person which was an amazing way to get around and see some wildlife. Sadly though this was the only way to see it as the path ends abruptly after 200m and that’s it. The restaurant is severely overpriced but the garden around is nice and pretty. I would recommend if you have an hour or two.

reviews.author: L L
reviews.text: Beautiful and breathtaking. Really worth the visit. If you can book a boat tour in the very morning. Then you can see a lot of different birds and hear their calls. Also has a nice botanical garden with many orchids.

reviews.author: Eleni C
reviews.text: Wonderful, peaceful place! We really enjoyed our time there and we are happy to give our money to a place that offers job opportunities to locals. We don’t consider the ticket expensive as other visitors mentioned.

reviews.author: Cindea Gigi
reviews.text: The presentation of this place made us to expect something wild, natural and unique. In fact is not more than a park, just few minutes of “sailing” by two boats, nothing very interesting to see. Few birds, a part of a mangrove forest, some flowers. A lot of people, it was like a huge picnik. Good for relax, for a contact with nature, for see some birds and tree species. Nothing special.

reviews.author: Alma Moises
reviews.text: We really struggled to find our way around this place, but thankfully a kind local motorcyclist escorted us. I'm so grateful for his help! It was a relatively quiet day since it was a Friday, which made the experience even more enjoyable. I absolutely loved being immersed in the wilderness and forest.
